auditTrail.empty: No audit events match your filters.
auditTrail.intro: Compliance activity log — who did what, when, and from where. Append-only store; export for auditors.
auditTrail.introKillSwitch: BWC reboot, shutdown, approvals, denials, and cancellations. Default range is the last 30 days — adjust dates or export CSV for compliance review.
auditTrail.noPerm: Audit trail access is not granted for your account.
auditTrail.introGeofenceReport: Geofence saves, clears, breaches, and re-entries. Default range is the last 30 days — adjust dates or export CSV for compliance review.
auditTrail.presetBannerKillSwitch: Scope: BWC reboot, shutdown, approvals, denials, and cancellations. Default date range: last 30 days.
auditTrail.presetBannerGeofence: Scope: geofence saves, clears, breaches, and re-entries. Default date range: last 30 days.
auditTrail.summaryKillSwitch: Kill switch report — showing {from}–{to} of {total} events · {store} total in store
auditTrail.summaryGeofence: Geofence report — showing {from}–{to} of {total} events · {store} total in store
auditTrail.summary: Showing {from}–{to} of {total} matching · {store} total in store
auditTrail.title: Audit Trail
bwc.alert.imported: Imported {n} BWC(s). Map groups set pin colours.
bwc.error.csvEmpty: No device IDs found in CSV
bwc.error.csvNoDeviceId: CSV must have a Device ID column
bwc.error.needDeviceId: Add at least one device ID
bwc.hint: Add every body-worn camera here — no limit. Set a nickname for each (what operators see on the map and device list). The long device ID comes from the BWC — paste it once here; you do not change it on the camera.
bwc.placeholder.deviceId: Long SIP ID — set on the camera, not changed here
bwc.placeholder.mapGroup: e.g. North patrol — same group = same pin colour
bwc.placeholder.nickname: e.g. Officer Lee — shown on map & devices list
bwc.title: All BWCs
call.title: Call BWC (Voice Intercom)
call.voiceBlockedLive: Stop live video to use phone. While live: Listen (speaker), hold PTT to talk.
caseFiles.createFirstConfirm: No case files yet. Create one and link this evidence?
caseFiles.deleteConfirm: Delete case file "{title}" ({id})? This cannot be undone.
caseFiles.deleteDone: Case file deleted. Evidence files on storage were not removed.
caseFiles.deleteEvidenceKept: Linked evidence files ({count}) stay in the Evidence Library and on server storage — only this case record and links are removed.
caseFiles.hint: Open a case file to write the field report and link evidence from the library.
caseFiles.selectCasePrompt: Enter case ID to link this evidence:
caseFiles.title: Title
centre.activity.name: Name
centre.activity.title: Recent Activity
centre.chart.title: SOS Trend
centre.liveViewers.title: Live viewers
centre.llm.failed: Could not get an answer. Try again.
centre.llm.placeholder: Ask about devices, SOS, or storage…
centre.llm.q1: How many devices are online right now?
centre.llm.q3: Is anything I should worry about?
centre.llm.title: AI assistant
centre.llm.welcome: Ask about devices online, open SOS alarms, trends, or storage.
centre.llm.willDownload: AI is included with your install — starting up…
centre.storage.title: Storage Breakdown
centre.svc.title: System Health
centre.title: Centre Summary
cloud.access.title: Public Access & Topology
cloud.arch.centralConsoleNote: Multi-site operations console for fleet-wide visibility across deployments
cloud.arch.dedicatedSiteNote: One organization per server — sovereign data boundary, standard for dispatch and public safety
cloud.arch.featureTiersNote: License entitlements control PTT, evidence, and live capture per organization
cloud.arch.sharedPlatformNote: Hosted platform serving multiple independent organizations on one URL
cloud.arch.storageQuotasNote: Central enforcement of per-organization evidence storage limits
cloud.arch.title: Platform Capabilities
cloud.arch.verificationNote: Available when a verification endpoint is configured
cloud.entitlement.installNote: Install a signed platform entitlement file on this server
cloud.firewall.hint: Required ports for cloud security groups and edge firewalls. Align with your Server tab SIP and media settings.
cloud.firewall.title: Inbound Services Checklist
cloud.intro: Hosted and hybrid operations — site profile, platform entitlements, secure operator access, and scheduled verification. SIP, LAN, and protocol settings remain on the Server tab.
cloud.loadFailed: Could not load cloud deployment settings
cloud.notes.placeholder: Handover contact, maintenance window, deployment notes…
cloud.notes.title: Deployment notes
cloud.ops.checkinHint: (configure as verification endpoint on each site)
cloud.ops.hint: Vendor toolkit commands run from your operations network, not on customer servers.
cloud.ops.title: Vendor Operations Toolkit
cloud.program.title: Deployment options
cloud.readiness.title: Site configuration status
cloud.saved: Saved. Restart Mobility if the operator URL or public address changed.
cloud.site.title: Site Identity
cloud.verification.hint: When enabled, this site checks in with your configured verification service on a schedule. Use it only when that service exists for the deployment.
cloud.verification.notConfigured: Not configured — enable scheduled verification and set a verification endpoint.
cloud.verification.title: Central Entitlement Verification
commandWall.meta: Drag Devices from the Roster Into Any Panel
commandWall.metaPopout: Drag devices from the roster into any panel · auto-starts live
commandWall.title: Command Wall
conference.bwcIngressUnavailable: Live body-camera share needs the conference service on this server. Ask your IT administrator to start it, then try again.
conference.bwcMaxReached: Maximum {max} BWC shares in this room
conference.bwcNoOnline: No body cameras online — phones and PC join with Join Room below.
conference.bwcOnlineOne: {count} body camera online — select it and tap Add to Room
conference.clientNotLoaded: Video client failed to load — refresh the page
conference.layoutDocumentOnly: Please choose a PDF or image document
conference.layoutDragHint: Drag to reorder or drop on shared pane
conference.layoutDropShare: Drop a participant here or share screen, video, document, or image
conference.layoutHint: Briefing: BWC and shares stay on the main stage; participants rotate in the strip when more than four. Gallery polls people the same way when the room is crowded. Hover the strip to pause rotation. Drag the divider to resize.
conference.layoutOverflow: +{n} more participants (pin or enlarge share to focus)
conference.layoutWaiting: Select a room and tap Join Room.
conference.lobbyHint: Online officers and BWCs by dispatch group pin colour. Hosts may invite guests across groups.
conference.marketingBlurb: Secure multi-party video for dispatch — field phones on mobile networks, server recording, and live body-camera sharing.
conference.noPerm: Video Conference Access Is Not Granted for Your Account.
conference.recDeleteConfirm: Delete this recording file from the server?
conference.recordingsEmpty: No recordings yet. Join a room, then use Record in Host Tools.
conference.recordingsPathHint: Recordings are stored on this server. Delete permanently removes the file.
conference.requestFailed: Request failed — try again or ask your administrator to restart Mobility.
conference.roomNotOpen: Room is not open yet — ask a host to start it.
conference.roomOpenHint: Room is open — tap Join Room to enter with your camera.
conference.rosterHint: Officers in the room are highlighted. Mobile joins appear when they tap Join Room on the app.
conference.serverNotReady: Conference is unavailable. Ask your administrator to restart Mobility, then refresh this page.
conference.settingsCredentialsServerNote: Video service credentials are configured on the server by your IT administrator. Operators only set addresses phones can reach.
conference.settingsDetectFail: Open Mobility using your server LAN IP (not localhost), then try again.
conference.settingsFormIntro: Set your site address and phone video URL below, then tap Test connection and Save.
conference.settingsFormIntroLan: Set your LAN address and phone video URL below. API credentials stay on the server — you do not need to enter them.
conference.settingsModeCloudHint: Use your cloud project URL and API keys. Set the public secure WebSocket URL from your provider dashboard.
conference.settingsModeLanHint: Phones on your LAN or VPN must reach this server. Set site host and media IP to that address — not 127.0.0.1.
conference.settingsModeRemoteHint: Video runs on another host. Set the API URL to that server; the phone URL must be what browsers and phones can reach.
conference.settingsMuteAllOnStartHint: When enabled, opening Room 1, 2, or 3 applies mute-all automatically. Host and super admins can still speak; others request the floor.
conference.settingsPublicWsHint: WebSocket URL for field phones and browsers — e.g. ws://192.168.1.38:7880
conference.settingsReadonly: View only — ask a conference host or super admin to change these settings.
conference.settingsReadyInternetNo: Internet joins need WSS, public media path or TURN, and open firewall UDP
conference.settingsReadyLanNo: Complete the items above for LAN / VPN joins
conference.settingsRestartNote: After saving, restart Mobility if phones still show the old video address.
conference.settingsSiteHostHint: Address phones and remote browsers use to reach Mobility — not 127.0.0.1.
conference.settingsTestOk: Video service reachable and API keys accepted.
conference.setupHint: Video conference is not set up on this server yet. Ask your IT administrator to configure the conference service.
conference.setupPublicUrl: Field phones cannot use localhost — set your LAN WebRTC URL before they join.
conference.setupRequired: Video conference MCU is not configured on this server.
conference.speakNotAllowed: Host has not allowed you to speak yet — tap Request to speak
conference.speakRequested: Speak request sent — waiting for host
displayRoom.autoScreens: Place windows on extended displays automatically
displayRoom.autoScreensHint: When checked, Chrome may ask to manage windows on other screens. If place fails, drag the window manually and press F11.
displayRoom.autoScreensUnsupported: This browser cannot auto-place windows. Leave the box unchecked and drag each window to the correct monitor, then F11.
displayRoom.placeFailNoApi: {monitor} window opened — this browser cannot auto-place. Drag it to the correct monitor and press F11.
displayRoom.placeFailNoApiLaunch: Windows opened — this browser cannot auto-place. Drag each to monitors 2–4 and press F11.
displayRoom.placeFailDenied: {monitor} window opened — screen permission denied or unavailable. Allow window management if prompted, or drag manually and press F11.
displayRoom.placeFailNoScreen: {monitor} window opened — no matching extended display. Connect/extend the monitor, or drag manually and press F11.
displayRoom.placeFailClosed: {monitor} window closed before it could be placed.
displayRoom.placeFailManual: {monitor} window opened — could not move it automatically. Drag to the correct monitor and press F11.
displayRoom.groupsHint: Dispatch groups (reference). Open all monitors does not auto-fill or auto-call — drag devices onto the wall when ready.
displayRoom.intro: Monitors 2–4 open in new windows. Drag each to the correct display and press F11. For a same-screen preview, use the Video wall tab above.
displayRoom.launchOk: Windows opened — drag each to displays 2–4 and press F11.
displayRoom.launchOkManual: Monitor windows opened (empty wall) — drag to displays 2–4 if needed, then F11. Drag devices to go live.
displayRoom.launchOkPlaced: Placed {n} window(s) on extended displays. Press F11 on each for fullscreen.
displayRoom.launchWarn: This window switches to Operations (desk). Opens empty video wall, map, and status board — same as monitors 2–4. Drag devices to go live; nothing auto-calls.
displayRoom.monitor2Hint: Live multi-panel video wall — opens in a new window on monitor 2.
displayRoom.monitor3Hint: Map mirror — opens in a new window on monitor 3.
displayRoom.monitor3HintAnalytics: Face watch pop-out — FR watch tiles; map stays on Operations.
displayRoom.monitor4Hint: Centre summary status board — opens in a new window on monitor 4.
displayRoom.noGroups: No dispatch groups — create them under Server → Map groups.
displayRoom.wallInTabFail: Could not open Video wall. Use the Video wall tab above, or reload.
displayRoom.wallInTabOk: Use the Video wall tab above for a same-window preview.
displayRoom.popupBlocked: Pop-up blocked for {list}. Use the blocked-pop-up icon in the address bar, choose Always allow for this site, then click again.
displayRoom.popupOpenedWall: Empty video wall opened — drag devices from the roster to go live. Close the window to clear this message.
displayRoom.popupOpenedPlaced: {monitor} window placed on extended display — press F11 for fullscreen.
displayRoom.popupOpenedAnalytics: Face watch window opened — check the taskbar or move it to monitor 3.
displayRoom.popupOpenedMap: Map window opened — check the taskbar or move it to monitor 3.
displayRoom.popupOpenedCentre: Status board window opened — check the taskbar or move it to monitor 4.
displayRoom.presetSosDesc: One click opens the same windows as monitors 2–4 (empty wall — no auto live).
displayRoom.selectGroup: Select at least one dispatch group for the TV wall.
displayRoom.step1: Windows → Display → Extend these displays (HDMI/USB-C adapters as needed).
displayRoom.step2: Click Open all monitors (or each monitor button), drag windows to displays 2–4, then F11. Drag devices onto the wall to go live.
displayRoom.step3: Keep monitor 1 on Operations — all SOS acknowledge and PTT control stays there.
displayRoom.step4: Allow pop-ups if the browser blocks new windows.
displayRoom.streamNote: One live stream per body-worn camera, shared by all operators. For control rooms: use different cameras on Operations and the video wall. The same camera on both is allowed but not recommended.
errors.accountExpired: This account has expired. Contact your administrator.
errors.accountNotActive: This account is not active yet. Check your sign-in from date.
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Try again or contact your IT administrator.
errors.geofenceClearRequired: Provide geofence data or clear the existing geofence.
errors.geofenceNotPermitted: Geofence control is not permitted for your account.
errors.groupNoDevices: This group has no devices with IDs.
errors.mapControlRequired: Map remote control permission is required.
errors.noGeofence: No geofence is set for this device.
errors.operationOverlayCloseRequired: Operation overlay close permission is required.
errors.operationOverlayEditRequired: Operation overlay edit permission is required.
errors.orgSignInRequired: Local sign-in is disabled. Use your organization account.
auth.passwordPolicy.hint: Min {min} characters · upper, lower, number, symbol · type example: {example}
errors.passwordChangeRequired: Change your password before using the dashboard.
errors.totpEnrollRequired: Set up your authenticator before using the dashboard.
errors.passwordConfirmRequired: Enter your password to confirm this change.
errors.permissionDenied: You do not have permission for this action.
errors.pttDisabled: Push-to-talk is not enabled on this server.
errors.pttNeedTwoUnits: Select at least two online units for group push-to-talk.
errors.pttSelectUnits: Select a map group or at least two online units.
errors.pttUnitBusy: This unit is already on the push-to-talk team.
errors.storageMountNotFound: Mount folder not found on this server. Ask IT to connect shared storage first.
errors.storageNasRequired: Shared storage path is required when using network archive.
errors.superAdminPasswordRequired: Your super admin password is required.
errors.usbWindowsOnly: USB maintenance requires Windows on the dispatch PC.
errors.userExists: That username is already in use. Pick another name or edit the existing row.
evidence.catalogErrorBusy: The evidence index is in use. Wait a moment and try again.
evidence.catalogErrorDamaged: The evidence index on this server could not be read. Contact your IT administrator to restore from backup or rebuild the index from archived files.
evidence.catalogErrorGeneric: The evidence index could not be accessed. Contact your IT administrator.
evidence.catalogErrorNotReady: The evidence index is still starting. Refresh in a moment.
evidence.catalogErrorUnavailable: The evidence index is not available. Contact your IT administrator.
evidence.installerNote: Ask IT to mount shared storage on this server, set the path above, then point dock uploads to the same folder.
evidence.installerNoteSan: Ask IT to mount the SAN volume on this server first, then enter the folder path above.
evidence.liveCaptureAutoOnSos: Auto-record server video on SOS alarm
evidence.nasMountHint: Before use: mount shared NAS storage on this Windows/Linux host, then point FTP upload or live capture at that mount.
evidence.pathsHint: Configure where dock FTP and server live capture files are stored. NAS paths are mounted by your IT team on this server — Mobility only writes to the folder you set here.
evidence.storageAdminRequired: Super admin required to change storage.
evidence.storageCatalogHint: Evidence IDs, audit trail, and device list — kept on this server. Video files stay in the archive above.
evidence.storageSanHint: Your IP SAN appears here after IT connects the LUN (Windows: assign a drive letter, e.g. E:\MobilityEvidence).
evidence.ftpCredentialsHint: Set credentials for body-worn cameras and docking stations. Enter the same values on each device FTP screen. Password is stored on this server only — not shown again after save.
evidence.ftpPasswordPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current password
evidence.ftpSavedRestarted: FTP settings saved and service restarted.
smtp.title: Outbound email (SMTP)
smtp.hint: Used for password reset and sign-in recovery (when enabled later). Configure once. Password is stored in the server secrets vault only.
smtp.passwordPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current password
smtp.testToRequired: Enter a test recipient email address.
smtp.adminRequired: Super admin access is required to change SMTP settings.
recoveryEmail.title: Recovery email
recoveryEmail.setupHint: Add an email for password reset and lost-authenticator recovery. We send a one-time link to verify it.
recoveryEmail.myAccountHint: Used for password reset and lost-authenticator recovery. Changing it requires a new verification email.
recoveryEmail.sent: Verification email sent to {email}. Open the link within 30 minutes.
recoveryEmail.pendingHint: Check your inbox at {email} and open the verification link.
recoveryEmail.smtpRequired: Outbound email is not configured yet. A super admin must set up SMTP under Settings → Server Config → Dashboard Auth.
recoveryEmail.notSet: Not set — add an email and send verification.
recoveryEmail.verifyInvalid: This verification link is invalid or expired.
recoveryEmail.verifySuccess: Recovery email verified: {email}. You can sign in to the dashboard.
recoveryEmail.verifyServerError: Could not reach the server. Try again later.
errors.recoveryEmailRequired: Recovery email verification is required before using the dashboard.
evidence.storageScanDone: FTP scan indexed {n} file(s) in the evidence index.
evidence.storageTestDone: Path test complete — see status badges.
evidence.storageTierHint: Video files stay on disk or shared storage. This server keeps the evidence index and audit records.
evidence.storageTierLocalHint: Default — local folder on this server
evidence.storageTierNetworkHint: Shared storage mounted by IT — SAN, NAS, or network drive
evidence.transcriptsHint: Speech-to-text for recorded SOS voice and video appears here and in incident detail — not on the live Operations map.
evidence.transcriptsNotAvailable: Speech-to-text is not enabled on this server. There are no transcripts to view.
evidenceHub.approvalsDisabledNote: Secure export is disabled on this server. Operators cannot queue protected exports until it is enabled.
evidenceHub.approvalsFlowLine: Operators request secure export from Evidence Library → you approve or deny here with your password → share the unlock code once, separately from the file.
evidenceHub.approvalsHint: Review export requests from operators. Confirm with your password. The unlock code is shown once — give it to the requester by phone or in person, not in email.
evidenceHub.approvalsNoRecent: No approved or denied export requests yet.
evidenceHub.approvalsStep1: An operator opens Evidence Library, selects a file, and chooses Request secure export.
evidenceHub.approvalsStep2: The request appears here for super-admin review. Approve or deny with your password.
evidenceHub.approvalsStep3: If approved, copy the unlock code once and share it separately from the encrypted file.
evidenceHub.approvalsSuperNote: Super administrators can also download protected files directly from the library without a queue request.
evidenceHub.catalogEmptyNote: Live SOS server capture also adds files when enabled on this server.
evidenceHub.catalogEmptyStep1: Body-worn cameras upload video when placed in a registered docking station.
evidenceHub.catalogEmptyStep2: Files land in the FTP ingest folder configured in Storage.
evidenceHub.catalogEmptyStep3: Super administrators can run Scan FTP in Storage to index existing files.
evidenceHub.catalogEmptyUnavailableLead: The evidence index on this server could not be read.
evidenceHub.catalogEmptyUnavailableNote: Open Storage to check paths, restore from backup, or run Scan FTP to rebuild the index.
evidenceHub.custodyEmpty: No custody events recorded for this file yet.
evidenceHub.custodyEmptyUnavailable: No custody events could be read for this file.
evidenceHub.custodyMissingNote: This file is missing from server storage. Removals performed outside the application are not attributed to a user. Each time an authorized user opens this record, the system logs that the file is missing.
evidenceHub.custodyUnavailable: The server audit database could not be read, so custody history is unavailable until IT restores mobility.db from backup or repairs the audit table.
evidenceHub.detailLoadFailed: This evidence record could not be opened. Refresh and try again. If it keeps failing, contact your IT administrator.
evidenceHub.dockFleetSummary: {up} site(s) online · {down} offline · {bays} of {total} bays occupied
evidenceHub.missingBody: This evidence file is no longer present in server storage. Contact your administrator to check transfer, archive, or deletion history.
evidenceHub.redactedExportsHint: Find finalized redacted copies without opening each source clip. Download here; open source to return to the original evidence.
evidenceHub.redactedExportsSearchPh: File name, officer, device, export ID…
evidenceHub.redactedExportsEmpty: No redacted exports match these filters.
evidenceHub.holdsHint: Face snaps held for investigation from live FR. Browse here — no need to open the server folder.
evidenceHub.holdsEmpty: No investigation holds yet. Use Keep on an FR snap or map pin to save one here.
evidenceHub.holdsNotLicensed: Face recognition is not licensed on this server.
evidenceHub.noDockPerm: Dock administration is not granted for your account.
evidenceHub.noDocks: No docking stations registered yet. Super admin or dock admin can register one.
evidenceHub.noViewPerm: Evidence access is not granted for your account.
evidenceHub.archiveConfirm: Remove from active library? File is kept in archive and can be restored.
evidenceHub.overviewGuidanceAdmin: Priority evidence retrieval: {library} search first · unindexed media: {storage}, Scan FTP · export release: {exportQueue}.
evidenceHub.overviewGuidanceOperator: Priority evidence retrieval: search {library} first. If the recording is not listed or export is pending approval, contact a super administrator on duty.
evidenceHub.overviewHint: Receive and archive status for your evidence index.
evidenceHub.overviewRecoveryAdmin: Evidence index unavailable — open {storage}, restore from backup, or run Scan FTP to rebuild from archived files.
evidenceHub.railHintApprovals: Pending exports and archive health. Approve requests in the main panel.
evidenceHub.railHintCaseFiles: Link evidence from the library. Index health reflects catalog availability.
evidenceHub.railHintCatalog: Index and fleet snapshot. Open Storage to change archive paths.
evidenceHub.redactHelp: Mark areas to blur on the video, then save a redacted copy. The original recording is not changed.
evidenceHub.redactHint: Pause the video, drag on the picture to mark blur areas, then set how long each area stays blurred and save.
evidenceHub.redactNoRegions: Mark at least one blur area before saving.
evidenceHub.redactNeedReason: Choose a redaction reason before saving.
evidenceHub.redactAutoFaceDone: {n} face area(s) added — review, adjust, or delete before saving.
evidenceHub.redactAutoFaceFollowDone: {n} face preview(s) on the picture — look, delete bad ones if needed, then Save. Save blurs faces through the clip (you do not type Start/End). Draw a box only for plates or extra.
evidenceHub.redactAutoFaceNone: No faces detected automatically — draw the areas manually.
evidenceHub.redactSaveProgress: Still working — {t} elapsed. Long clips can take several minutes.
evidenceHub.redactSaveCancelled: Save cancelled. You can try again or adjust areas.
evidenceHub.redactSaveTimeout: Save timed out after 12 minutes. Try a shorter clip or fewer areas, then Save again.
evidenceHub.redactSaveReady: Redacted copy ready. Use Finalize & register (or Close and open Prior exports — do not Save again).
evidenceHub.redactSaveNoExport: Save finished but no export was returned. Close and check Prior exports, or try Save once more.
evidenceHub.redactDraftHint: Fill details now (before or while Save runs). You do not wait for burn to finish to type these.
evidenceHub.redactNoteHint: Describe what remains visible after blur — not identity guesses.
evidenceHub.redactIncidentPh: e.g. approached patrol car from the left
evidenceHub.previewLockedBody: This {kind} is hidden by default. Open preview only when needed.
evidenceHub.routeTraceHint: GPS trail by segment. Select a point to open matching evidence when available.
evidenceHub.sdkPending: Station link pending — live bay status, uploads, and sign-in appear when the docking station is connected to this site.
evidenceHub.secureRequested: Your request was submitted ({id}). A super admin will review it.
evidenceHub.storageNote: Recorded video stays on local disk or shared storage. This server holds the evidence index, audit trail, and device list.
evidenceHub.storageRailHint: Live ingest and index health. Save or test paths on the left to update archive settings.
evidenceHub.trimHint: Type the In/Out seconds directly, or open the preview, scrub, and click Use playhead to capture the current time. A new trimmed clip is created — the original recording is not changed.
evidenceHub.trimUsePlayheadTitle: Copy the video's current time into this box
evidenceHub.trimLenToEnd: Clip length: In → end of clip
evidenceHub.trimOpenVideoFirst: Open the video preview and scrub to the moment first, then use the playhead.
missedActivity.title: Missed activity
missedActivity.pttDetail: Field PTT ended before an operator engaged the banner.
missedActivity.fallDetail: Fall alert recorded while you were away.
missedActivity.sosDetail: SOS recorded while you were away.
firmware.dock.note: Firmware can also be updated on the vendor docking station when officers return cameras at end of shift. Evidence upload from the dock uses FTP settings under Evidence → Settings.
firmware.dock.title: Docking Station Upgrades
firmware.fleet.empty: No cameras registered yet — add BWCs on the BWCs tab.
firmware.fleet.hint: Current version per camera from live status reports. Offline units show the last version received.
firmware.fleet.title: Mobility Firmware Status
firmware.hint: See firmware versions across your Mobility. For over-the-air upgrades, work with your BWC vendor — they register supported models and supply approved firmware packages.
firmware.phases.title: How Upgrades Work
firmware.profiles.empty: No models registered yet. Contact your BWC vendor to enable firmware OTA for your cameras.
firmware.profiles.hint: Camera models registered by your vendor for over-the-air upgrade are listed here. Ask your vendor to add or update a model for your Mobility.
firmware.profiles.title: Supported Camera Models
firmware.title: Firmware OTA
firmware.versions.label: Firmware Versions in Mobility:
firmware.versions.none: No version data yet — ensure cameras are online and reporting status.
fleet.addNicknameHint: Add nickname in Server Config → BWCs
fleet.clearMapPinsConfirm: Clear all map pins? This closes pin popups and removes markers from the map.
fleet.clearMapPinsNoPerm: Clear map pins requires super admin or permission from Dashboard Auth.
fleet.searchAria: Search devices by name or ID
fleet.title: Devices
geofence.clear.hint: Confirm with your dashboard sign-in, then choose which registered BWC loses its geofence (offline is OK).
geofence.clear.noSavedHint: No saved geofence on any registered BWC — use Set geofencing first, then Save.
geofence.draw.hint: Drag orange centre dot · drag white edge dot for size · or click map
geofence.error.needCredentials: Enter username and password (same as dashboard sign-in).
geofence.error.noGeofence: That BWC has no saved geofence. Use Set geofencing first.
geofence.error.notPermitted: This account is not allowed to set or clear geofences.
geofence.error.selectBwc: Select a BWC from the list.
geofence.error.selectGeofenced: Select a BWC that has a saved geofence (Geofenced tag).
geofence.set.empty: No BWCs match this filter. Add devices in Server Config → BWCs.
geofence.set.hint: Confirm with your dashboard sign-in (same as server login). Pick a registered BWC — offline is OK for pre-setting before they come online.
geofence.toast.centreMoved: Centre moved — drag white dot for size or Save
geofence.toast.drawStart: Geofence for {name}: drag orange centre · white edge for size
groups.colorHint: Click a swatch or the colour square. Pins update when you press Save group.
groups.csvColorHint: CSV colour column: colour name (green, blue, amber, purple, red, cyan, pink, yellow, indigo, teal), number 1–10, or hex code (#22c55e). Leave blank for automatic colour.
groups.empty: No groups yet — add one or import CSV.
groups.hint: Name teams, set pin colour, and add members (nickname + device ID). Upload CSV for bulk setup. Saving updates map pin colours and BWC map groups.
groups.membersEmpty: No members yet — click + Add member or choose a BWC in the row dropdown.
groups.name: Group name
groups.steps: 1. Name and pin colour · 2. Add members · 3. Save group
groups.title: Dispatch Groups
groups.viewHint: Members in this group (map pin colour matches the dot).
lab.intro: Identity provider and monitoring — elevated access required. Live video and SOS are unchanged.
lab.proxyHint: Portal HTTPS / Trust reverse proxy: Server → Reverse proxy.
lab.savedRestart: Saved. Restart Mobility if proxy trust settings changed.
lab.oidc.enable: Enable OIDC on the operator sign-in page
lab.oidc.autoProvision: Auto-provision new IdP users as operators
lab.notes.placeholder: Site, bid reference, IdP realm notes…
lang.label: Language
live.duplicateSurfaceWarn: Camera {camId} is live on Operations and the video wall in this window. For control rooms, use different cameras on each screen when possible.
login.title: Mobility Axiom
login.totpHint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
login.totpBackupHint: Or enter a one-time backup code.
login.hintPassword: First install: password is global123. If you already changed it, use your new password — global123 will fail. Type it; do not rely on a saved browser password.
totpEnroll.backupHint: Save these backup codes once. Each works one time if you lose your phone.
totpEnroll.error: Could not complete setup. Check your password and code.
totpEnroll.scanHint: Scan this QR in your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code.
totpEnroll.startHint: Super admin must use an authenticator app (Microsoft Authenticator, Google Authenticator, etc.). Works offline — no internet needed for codes.
totpEnroll.title: Set up authenticator
mustChangePassword.error: Could not change password. Check your entries.
mustChangePassword.exampleHint: Rules: at least 12 characters, with upper case, lower case, a number, and a symbol. Example you can type: Ab12cd34!@#$
mustChangePassword.hint: Current password for first change is the install password: global123. You cannot keep that password. Choose a new one that meets the rules below.
mustChangePassword.pasteHint: Do not paste into password fields — type the new password.
mustChangePassword.title: Change your password
map.country.label: Map region
map.fitPinsTitle: Zoom map to show all pinned devices (or all on map if none pinned)
map.placeSearch.offline: Place search needs internet — pan/zoom manually or use Fit pins.
map.placeSearch.rateLimited: Too many searches — wait a moment.
map.offline.pmtiles3dTitle: Toggle offline PMTiles 3D building overlay (synced with map)
map.attribution.offline: © OpenStreetMap contributors · Carto · offline tiles (local)
map.attribution.online: © OpenStreetMap · OpenMapTiles
map.geofencePermDenied: Geofencing permission not granted for your account.
map.gpsPending: Awaiting GPS: {names} — enable GPS on BWC
map.permDenied: Remote control permission not granted for your account.
map.permGrantSteps: Ask your super admin to open Server Config → Dashboard Auth, set your row, and click Save.
map.permHint: Remote control permission not granted. Click Permission for how to get access.
map.pin.wallStillLive: Pin closed — video still live on wall
map.popoutMirrorHint: Display only — control from main dashboard
map.serverRecordStoppedPending: Server recording stopped. File is being finalized.
map.confirmLock: Lock BWC {id}? The officer cannot use the device until unlock.
map.confirmReboot1: Reboot BWC {id}? Live video and recording will stop.
map.confirmReboot2: Confirm reboot — the device will restart now.
map.confirmShutdown1: Shut down BWC {id}? The device will power off.
map.confirmShutdown2: Confirm shut down — power on again at the device.
map.killSwitchDenied: Shut down and reboot require the device kill switch permission.
map.killSwitch.reasonHint: Reason is required for the audit log (minimum 10 characters). Ticket reference is optional.
map.killSwitch.reasonTooShort: Enter at least {min} characters explaining this action.
map.killSwitch.fourEyesReasonHint: Reboot and shut down need a second operator to approve. Enter a reason (minimum 10 characters). Ticket reference is optional.
map.killSwitch.fourEyesRequested: Kill switch request submitted. A second operator must approve within 5 minutes.
map.killSwitch.fourEyesApproved: Kill switch approved and command sent.
map.killSwitch.confirmApprove: Approve this kill switch command and send it to the BWC now?
map.killSwitch.confirmCancel: Cancel your pending kill switch request?
map.telemetry.title: Device Status
map.toolbar.selectBwcTitle: Select online BWC, then use Snapshot or SD record
map.toolbar.selectFirst: Select an online BWC from the list first.
matrix.noVideo: No live video on this panel
matrix.openerClosed: Dashboard window closed — close this matrix.
matrix.title: Video Matrix
messages.clearConfirm: Clear messages for this BWC? This cannot be undone.
messages.clearFailed: Could not clear messages. Restart the fleet server and try again.
messages.pickAlert: Select a BWC above to send a message
messages.retentionHint: Showing last 24 hours · stored up to 30 days
messages.title: Messages
analytics.gateBody: Face recognition, ANPR, and weapon detection are optional modules. Contact your administrator to enable them on this server.
analytics.popoutDeskHint: Map and dispatch stay on the Operations dashboard
analytics.fr.layoutHint: 6 live tiles · up to 32 in watch set · rotate ~20s (all selected get scanned)
analytics.fr.tileLiveCap: Server live limit — pause other views
analytics.fr.watchStub: Select up to 32 online BWCs. Only 4 show live at a time (saves decode). Face scan runs on the live tiles; rotate brings every selected BWC onto a tile so all get scanned over time — not only the first 4.
analytics.fr.stopVideoNeedFocus: Click a live tile first, then Stop video
analytics.fr.stopAllConfirm: Stop all video and clear the watch set?
analytics.fr.clearWatchConfirm: Stop video and clear all selected BWCs?
analytics.fr.pinHint: Pin to a live tile (skip rotate)
analytics.fr.subjectMatchesSub: Watchlist · threshold met · not all alerts
analytics.fr.snapPlayGone: Video no longer available. Load the file again.
analytics.fr.snapKeptFolderHint: Saved to Investigation holds (Evidence tab). IT path: {folder}.
analytics.fr.snapKeepNoCrop: No face crop for this pin — open the snap again to Keep
analytics.fr.snapKeepCropGone: Crop expired — open the snap again, then Keep
analytics.fr.snapKeepNotLicensed: Face recognition is not licensed — cannot Keep
analytics.fr.alertDrawerVideoHint: Live stream connects in a later update
analytics.fr.previewDrawerLabHint: Layout preview only — not a real match
analytics.fr.popoutGoOpsNoDesk: Open Operations on the main dashboard to view the map.
analytics.fr.blacklistStub: Open the Watchlist tab to enroll ID photos.
analytics.fr.watchlistStub: Open the Watchlist tab to enroll ID photos.
analytics.fr.alarmFieldFail: Field alert failed — BWC not on PTT or no SIP contact
analytics.fr.alarmFieldCooldown: Wait a few seconds before alerting again
analytics.fr.standbyPttTeamTitle: Push catching unit and nearby online BWCs to one PTT group (audio only)
analytics.fr.standbyPttTeamOn: Standby PTT ON — {n} unit(s): {names}. Hold PTT on map or wall.
analytics.fr.standbyPttTeamOk: Standby PTT ON — {names} ({n} units). Hold PTT to talk.
analytics.fr.standbyPttNoNearby: No online nearby units within 500 m.
analytics.fr.standbyPttNearestConfirm: No unit inside 500 m. Push standby PTT to nearest ({name}, {dist} m GPS)?
analytics.verify.hint: Compare two photos (for example an ID photo and a field photo). Face matching must be running on this server.
analytics.verify.serviceDown: Face matching is not available. Ask your administrator to start the face recognition service.
analytics.verify.notLicensed: Face recognition is not licensed on this server.
analytics.verify.noFace: No face found in one or both photos. Use a clear, front-facing picture.
analytics.verify.qualityLow: Photo quality is too low for a reliable check. Try a sharper, well-lit image.
analytics.verify.busy: Face matching is busy. Wait a moment and try again.
analytics.verify.badFile: Use a JPEG/PNG ID photo, or Add recent snapshots from BWC.
analytics.verify.timeout: Face matching took too long. Try again with smaller photos.
analytics.verify.failed: Face matching could not complete this check. Try again or contact your administrator.
analytics.verify.network: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
analytics.bl.hint: Enroll people on the Watchlist from an ID or clear photo. Choose a watch grade (POI → Blacklist). Up to 5,000 active entries. Face matching must be running.
analytics.bl.qualityHint: Use a clear, front-facing ID or mugshot. The face should fill much of the frame. JPEG or PNG. A large file is not enough if the face is tiny in the picture. Blurry or group photos will be rejected.
analytics.bl.name: Display name
analytics.bl.needReasonOther: Enter a short reason when Other is selected.
analytics.bl.cropHint: Frame one face. Green meters match our enroll rules. Built-in tool only — no third-party crop library.
analytics.bl.cropReady: Drag the frame onto the face, then Check or Use this crop.
analytics.bl.cropFirst: Crop & check the face first, then Add to watchlist.
analytics.bl.cropNeedFace: Enlarge the frame so the face area is at least 160 px.
analytics.bl.cropFallback: Crop tool unavailable — using full image.
analytics.bl.meterNote: Approx meters are local. “Check with face service” uses the same gate as enroll.
analytics.bl.matchDebugNoSnap: No live face snap yet. Start watch, get a face on Recent, then try again.
analytics.bl.matchDebugNoPrint: This person has no face fingerprint yet. Use Re-embed gallery, then try again.
analytics.bl.migrateConfirm: Re-embed all watchlist faces for the current FR engine? A backup of the gallery index is saved first. Photos are unchanged. Failures keep their old vectors.
analytics.bl.migrateDone: Gallery re-embed done: {migrated} updated, {skipped} skipped, {failed} failed (engine {engine}).
analytics.bl.migrateFail: Gallery re-embed failed. Check that the face service is running.
analytics.bl.confirmRemove: Remove this person from the watchlist?
analytics.bl.multiFace: More than one face was found. Use a photo with only one person, or crop to a single face.
analytics.bl.faceTooSmall: The face in this photo is too small. Crop closer so the face fills more of the picture, then try again.
analytics.bl.imageTooSmall: This photo is too low-resolution. Use a clearer, larger photo (at least about 480×480 pixels).
analytics.bl.qualityBlur: This photo looks too blurry for a reliable enroll. Use a sharper picture.
analytics.bl.qualityLighting: This photo is too dark or too bright on the face. Use a clearer, evenly lit picture.
analytics.bl.full: Watchlist is full. Disable or remove an entry before adding another.
analytics.bl.notFound: That watchlist entry was not found.
ptt.bannerLinger: 🎙 Field PTT — {name} (click for comm pin · Hold 🎙 to reply)
ptt.commHint: Click banner or fleet row to open comm pin — Hold 🎙 to reply
ptt.commHintCw: Hold the button below to talk back on PTT
ptt.commLinger: Transmission ended — Hold 🎙 below to reply
ptt.commReply: Hold 🎙 on this pin to reply
ptt.fallbackVoiceHold: Hold 🎙 to talk via voice call — {name}
ptt.groupBox.active: Group PTT active — {name} ({n} units). Hold PTT on any member.
ptt.groupBox.failed: PTT group push failed. Is PTT enabled?
ptt.groupBox.hint: Pick a coloured map group, or tick 2+ online units on the fleet list. Join groups them on one PTT channel; Ungroup restores default channels.
ptt.groupBox.pickFirst: Select a map group or tick at least 2 online units on the fleet list (Server → Map groups to create named groups).
ptt.groupBox.pickNeedTwo: Need 2+ selected ({n} now) — use + to add back
ptt.groupBox.pickNeedTwoAlert: Select at least 2 members for group PTT (× excludes someone from the roster).
ptt.groupBox.pinnedNeedMore: Tick one more online unit for ad-hoc group PTT (max 6 pins).
ptt.groupBox.pinnedReady: Pinned units ready — {n} selected (Join uses pins when no group is selected).
ptt.groupBox.title: PTT Groups
ptt.label: PTT
ptt.notOnChannel: BWC not on PTT channel yet
ptt.wakeFailed: BWC not on PTT channel yet — try again or press PTT on the BWC
resilience.hint: For a single site, leave peer settings empty. Enter a peer address when a second server is deployed for redundancy.
resilience.title: Site Resilience
server.alert.saved: Changes saved. Restart Mobility so cameras and operators use the new details.
server.alert.userExists: User {name} already exists — check the table below.
server.bindHostHint: 0.0.0.0 listens on all interfaces. Set a specific LAN IP only when the server has multiple NICs.
server.bwcGroupHint: Pick a map group per BWC (pin colour comes from Map groups tab). Create groups there with name, pin colour, and members.
server.bwcRegisterHint: IPv4 on each BWC SIP screen (keypads cannot use hostnames). Independent of the Operator portal URL.
server.bwcTabHint: Register every body-worn camera — one row per officer. Device ID is the long SIP ID from the camera. Nickname is what dispatch sees on the map and device list.
server.checklist.none: None yet — open Server Config → BWCs and add device IDs
server.secrets.passwordPlaceholder: Type new password to set or change
server.copyOperatorUrl.empty: Set an Operator portal URL first.
server.copyOperatorUrl.failed: Could not copy URL. Select the field and copy manually.
server.deploymentHint.cloud: Cloud VPS: set Device registration IPv4 to the public address BWCs dial. Set Operator portal URL to the HTTPS hostname staff bookmark. Open SIP and live-video ports in your cloud firewall.
server.deploymentHint.hybrid: Hybrid: Operator portal URL is the cloud HTTPS bookmark; Device registration IPv4 is what on-site BWCs reach via VPN or port-forward (SIP and media).
server.deploymentHint.lab: Lab: one PC on a test LAN. Portal URL and device IP may share the same host for simplicity — still keep the two fields separate.
server.deploymentHint.lan: LAN server: set static IP, subnet, gateway, and DNS to match the host OS. BWCs use Device registration IPv4 on the SIP screen; staff bookmark Operator portal URL.
server.diagnostics.hint: System diagnostics — administrator PIN required. Dashboard passwords cannot access this tab.
server.dock.envNote: Dock uploads use FTP on this server. If the service shows Stopped, contact your IT administrator to enable FTP and allow file transfer through your network firewall.
server.dock.hint: Docking stations and BWCs upload video over FTP to this server. Use the same server IP and FTP password as configured on each device.
server.dock.savedRestart: Upload folder saved. Restart Mobility for docks to use the new location.
server.dock.stopped: Stopped — contact your IT administrator to enable dock uploads
server.dock.uploadFolderHint: Folder on this server where docks drop video. Use the default or a path on shared storage. Restart Mobility after changing.
server.dockStorageHint: FTP upload, archive paths, and live capture are configured under Evidence → Storage.
server.error.ipv4Only:
Device registration must be IPv4 only (digits and dots). Body-worn keypads cannot enter hostnames.

Use Operator portal URL for staff HTTPS bookmarks.
server.gate.title: Confirm password
server.ipv4OnlyHint: Dynamic-SIM BWCs cannot type hostnames — digits and dots only. Your cloud/LAN/VPN fixed IP goes here.
server.lan.hint: On-prem server addressing. Configure the OS network adapter to match, or use DHCP and record the assigned address here.
server.lan.title: LAN Network
server.layoutHintAdmin: Full admin view — best for operators, BWCs, and permissions.
server.layoutHintCompact: Network & deployment tab — LAN, SIP, and operator URL. Expand for wider forms.
server.layoutHintWide: Wide view — more room for tables and lists.
server.myAccount.hint: You only see your own account here. A super admin manages permissions and passwords for other operators.
server.networkHint.cloud: Use your cloud VPS public IPv4. Port-forward SIP (5060) and UDP media to this server. Give renters that IP for every BWC.
server.networkHint.lan: Use static LAN IPv4 (e.g. 192.168.x.x). BWCs on the same network enter this IP on the SIP screen.
server.networkHint.vpn: Use the VPN gateway or tunnel endpoint IPv4 that SIM cameras reach. Still IP-only on the BWC — no DNS.
server.operatorDeviceSplit: Portal URL and device registration stay independent.
server.operatorSectionHint: HTTPS bookmark for dispatch staff. Not the device registration IPv4.
server.productionAccess.loadFailed: Could not load reverse proxy settings. Restart the server or contact your IT administrator.
server.productionAccess.saved: Saved. Restart Mobility if the proxy path was already live.
server.productionAccessHint: Terminate TLS on a reverse proxy in front of this app. Enable Trust reverse proxy when the portal URL is HTTPS.
server.password.changeHint: Any signed-in user can change their own password.
server.prompt.newPassword: New password for this user (min 12 · e.g. Ab12cd34!@#$):
server.readonlyBanner: Operator view — Server Config is read-only. Ask a super admin to change deployment or SIP.
server.restartNote: After saving network or SIP changes in Server Config, restart Mobility so cameras and operators pick up the new details.
server.setupHint: Network and SIP in Server Config apply to every BWC. Register each camera on the BWCs tab with its device ID and officer name.
server.siteTimezoneIntro: Used for audit timestamps, evidence index times, and operator sign-in windows.
server.sizingCalcHint: Estimates bandwidth, storage, and VM requirements for deployment planning. Import the CSV into your spreadsheet application.
server.summary.empty: No BWCs in list yet — open Server Config → BWCs or import CSV.
server.summary.loadError: Open Server Config → BWCs to add cameras (names and map groups).
server.summary.registered: {all} BWC(s) registered · {wall} on video wall · online units appear in Devices when connected
server.techGate.hint: IT administrator PIN — not your login password. Set under Settings → Server Config → Dashboard Auth.
server.techGate.title: Administrator Access
server.title: Server
server.typeOnBwcHint: Enter these server values on each BWC SIP screen. Device ID and operator name come from your BWC list.
server.users.passwordBothRequired: Enter the new password and your password to confirm.
server.users.permHint: Set each operator's authority below, then click Save on that row. Sign-in from/until, geofencing, and evidence until apply as listed.
server.users.removeConfirm: Remove account "{name}"? They will not be able to sign in.
server.users.setPasswordHint: Enter a new password for this operator. Confirm with your own super admin password.
server.users.superAdminCount: Super admin accounts: {count} of {max}. Keep at least two active for shift coverage.
server.users.title: Dashboard Users
server.users.unsavedHint: Unsaved changes — click Save on the row.
server.wan.hint: Public reachability for SIM cameras and remote operators. Configure router port-forward, cloud security group, or VPN to match.
server.wan.title: WAN / Internet
settingsHub.lifecycleLead: Register cameras, manage assets, and open Server Config or other admin pages. Status refreshes each time you open Settings.
settingsHub.status.configGeneric: Deployment and network — open Server Config
settingsHub.status.faultsCentre: SOS trends and fleet health in Centre Summary
settingsHub.status.faultsOps: Live SOS and alarms on the Operations map
settingsHub.status.firmwarePlanning: Inventory and planning — remote push not enabled yet
settingsHub.status.maintenanceFtpOff: Dock FTP is stopped — contact IT to enable uploads
settingsHub.status.maintenanceFtpOn: Dock FTP running — evidence paths under Storage
adminAction.wait: Please wait — finishing the current step.
adminAction.adminRequired: Super admin access is required for this action.
adminAction.toolsLoading: Admin tools are still loading — try again in a moment.
adminAction.diagnosticsSteps: Step 2 of 2: enter the IT administrator PIN.
adminAction.diagnosticsStep1: Step 1 of 2: enter your login password.
sos.ack.helpersEmpty: No other online units — acknowledge without a response team.
sos.ack.helpersHint: For the incident report only. Use PTT team on the SOS banner during the alarm for radio talk — you can save this form when the scene is clear.
sos.ack.includeSnapshot: Save this picture with the incident report
sos.ack.noVideo: No live video on screen — report will be saved without a picture.
sos.ack.title: SOS Acknowledgement
sos.banner.liveHint: If live video was on: press SOS on the BWC again if video does not start.
sos.banner.navHint: You can switch tabs anytime — Acknowledge when you take ownership.
sos.banner.pttTeamTitle: Push PTT group to alarm officer + nearby units
sos.confirm.clearList:
Clear the SOS list on this screen?

Saved incident folders on this PC are kept.
sos.detail.noReport: Report file not found for this incident. Try Reload list or open the server folder.
sos.detail.notAcknowledged: This alarm has not been acknowledged yet.
sos.detail.title: SOS Incident
sos.hint: Last 7 days — click a row for report · older: Open incident files
sos.ledger.serverError: Could not load — check server is running
sos.pin.hint: Full incident notes are protected. Enter the incident PIN from your administrator.
sos.pin.label: PIN
sos.pin.title: SOS Log — Enter PIN
sos.response.noGps: Waiting for officer GPS — adjust radius when location appears
sos.response.noneNearby: No other online units within {radius} m
sos.title: SOS Log
storage.ftpHint: FTP uploads — set paths in Evidence → Storage
storage.hint: FTP uploads folder on this PC
storage.title: Storage
tech.invalidPin: The administrator PIN is not correct.
tech.lockedOut: Too many attempts. Wait 15 minutes and try again.
tech.loadFailed: Could not load diagnostics. Try again or contact your IT administrator.
tech.notConfigured: Engineer diagnostics is not available on this server. Contact your Ubitron support team or IT administrator to enable access.
tech.provision.failed: Could not save the administrator PIN. Try again.
tech.provision.hint: Choose a PIN (12+ characters). Must be different from your login password. Confirm with your login password. Only a super admin can set this.
tech.provision.pinMismatch: The PIN entries do not match.
tech.provision.pinTooShort: The administrator PIN must be at least 12 characters.
tech.provision.saved: IT administrator PIN saved. Use it on Diagnostics Step 2.
tech.provision.title: Set IT administrator PIN
tech.health.title: Platform health
tech.liveViewers.title: Live viewer telemetry
tech.liveViewers.loadFailed: Could not load live viewer data.
tech.activity.title: Activity recording
tech.activity.note: System events are stored on the server. Contact your Ubitron support team if you need a log export.
tech.runbook.title: Guided checks
usbMaint.adbMissing: Device connection tool not available — contact your IT administrator.
usbMaint.adminPasswordPrompt: Your super admin password to confirm:
usbMaint.clearNotConfigured: Automatic media wipe is not enabled on this server.
usbMaint.clearNotConfiguredDetail:
To clear camera storage from this PC:

1. Connect the BWC by USB (Refresh USB must show the device).
2. Ask your IT administrator to enable automated wipe for your camera model.

Until then, use Open BWC tool to delete files with the vendor utility.
usbMaint.confirmClear: Clear media on this device? This cannot be undone.
usbMaint.fixConnection: Check the USB cable and accept any trust prompt on the camera.
usbMaint.hint: Super admin only. Connect the body-worn camera to this dispatch PC with USB, then refresh. Officers never install tools on this PC.
usbMaint.launchToolConfirm: Open the vendor maintenance tool on this PC? Use it to delete media until automated wipe is enabled.
usbMaint.noDevices: No camera connected — plug in the BWC and accept any trust prompt on the device.
usbMaint.pickDevice: Connect and select a USB device first.
usbMaint.title: USB Maintenance (Command Room)
usbMaint.toolLaunched: Vendor tool launched on this PC.
video.matrix.hint: Select wall panels (1–6). The pop-out arranges them in a standard grid (up to 1080p).
video.matrix.hintCw: Select Command Wall panels (1–16 or up to 32). The pop-out mirrors live video — no extra streams.
video.matrix.title: Pop Out Video Matrix
video.rotateHint: Panels set to rotate will cycle through online BWCs on the interval you configured.
video.title: Live Video
video.wall.allHint: Cycles through every online BWC registered on the BWCs tab — for large Mobility (hundreds or thousands).
video.wall.error.csvNoDeviceId: CSV must have a Device ID column
video.wall.error.csvNoIds: No device IDs found in CSV
video.wall.fixedHint: Paste the device ID from the BWCs tab. Use this for one dedicated panel.
video.wall.groupHint: Rotates through online BWCs in this map group (e.g. yellow patrol, north sector). Set map group on each row in Server Config → BWCs.
video.wall.hint: Six live panels. Register all BWCs in Server Config → BWCs. Here choose a fixed camera or a rotation (map group, all online, or paste a device ID list).
video.wall.hintNoDevice: Choose a source mode and configure this panel.
video.wall.hintUnknownId: {id} — add this ID in Server Config → BWCs.
video.wall.listHint: Paste device IDs one per line — BWC 7, 8, 9… up to thousands. Only online units play.
video.wall.overflowHint: Rotates through online BWCs that are not assigned to fixed panels 1–4. Use for panels 5–6 when you have more than four units.
video.wall.title: Video Wall Panels
view.popoutTitle: Pop out to another monitor or TV wall
voiceAlerts.config.adminRequired: Super admin required to save voice settings.
voiceAlerts.config.hint: Browser text-to-speech for control-room alerts. Click anywhere on the dashboard once to allow audio. Session mute is in the header.
voiceAlerts.config.speakRecStart: Speak when SD recording starts on device
voiceAlerts.config.speakRecStop: Speak when SD recording stops on device
voiceAlerts.config.title: Alerts & Voice
server.users.scopeCorporateHint: Sees all dispatch groups on map, fleet, and SOS ledger.
server.users.scopeStationHint: Only assigned dispatch groups on map, fleet, and SOS ledger.
server.users.hierarchyAssignHint: Assign dispatch groups below, or check See all dispatch groups.
server.users.hierarchyIntro: All stations = super admin or See all dispatch groups. Assigned only = pick groups in the Dispatch groups column.
server.readiness.title: Site readiness
server.readiness.hint: Deployment health before go-live. Click a row to open the setting.
server.readiness.loadFailed: Could not load readiness checklist. Restart the server or contact your IT administrator.
server.readiness.operatorUrl.label: Operator portal URL
server.readiness.operatorUrl.missing: Set the operator portal URL (bookmark for dispatch staff).
server.readiness.operatorUrl.missingRemo: Cloud/hybrid requires an operator portal URL.
server.readiness.operatorUrl.httpWarn: Use https:// for cloud/hybrid production access.
server.readiness.deviceIp.label: Device registration IPv4
server.readiness.deviceIp.ok: Valid IPv4 for BWC SIP registration.
server.readiness.license.label: Platform license
server.readiness.license.ok: License optional in this lab install.
server.readiness.license.optionalLab: No license file — OK for lab; required for rental/customer ship.
server.readiness.license.invalid: License file present but invalid or expired.
server.readiness.dispatchGroups.label: Dispatch groups
server.readiness.dispatchGroups.none: Create at least one dispatch group for map and SOS scope.
server.readiness.operators.label: Station-scoped operators
server.readiness.operators.ok: {operators} operator account(s); {assigned} with assigned groups only.
server.readiness.operators.onlySuperAdmi: Only super admin accounts — add operators for station-scoped desks.
server.readiness.operators.noAssigned: No operators with assigned groups — assign groups or use See all dispatch groups.
server.readiness.storage.label: Evidence ingest folder
server.readiness.storage.ok: FTP/evidence ingest folder exists and is writable.
server.readiness.storage.fail: Evidence ingest folder missing or not writable — open Evidence → Storage.
server.readiness.production.label: HTTPS / reverse proxy
server.readiness.production.ok: Production access settings look OK for this deployment mode.
server.readiness.production.trustProxy: HTTPS portal URL set — enable Trust reverse proxy behind nginx/IIS/Caddy.
server.readiness.production.https: Cloud/hybrid should use https:// on the operator portal URL.
server.readiness.production.trustProxyLa: HTTPS portal with direct app port — enable Trust reverse proxy if TLS terminates in front.
server.commandDisplays.title: Control room
server.commandDisplays.hint: Assign monitors for the control room — operator desk, video wall, map, and status board.
tech.adminPin.title: IT administrator PIN
tech.adminPin.hint: Separate from login (must be different). Required for Diagnostics and Lab. Super admin sets it for IT use — re-enter login password each time you change it.
tech.adminPin.statusSet: Status: PIN is set on this server.
tech.adminPin.statusNotSet: Status: not set yet — set a PIN before opening Diagnostics.
tech.provision.pinSameAsLogin: IT administrator PIN cannot be the same as your login password. Choose a different PIN.
authAudit.checklist.title: Auth & audit — before ship
authAudit.checklist.hint: Lab may show amber on TOTP while testing. Customer ship needs green (or fix each row). Click a row to open the setting.
authAudit.checklist.loadFailed: Could not load auth checklist. Restart the server or try again.
authAudit.checklist.totp.label: Authenticator (TOTP)
authAudit.checklist.totp.benchSuspended: Bench: TOTP paused (FM_TOTP_SUSPENDED). Remove this flag before customer ship.
authAudit.checklist.totp.shipReady: TOTP is on and this super admin has enrolled an authenticator.
authAudit.checklist.totp.enrollNeeded: TOTP is on for ship — enroll authenticator for this super admin (My account / first login).
authAudit.checklist.smtp.label: Outbound email (SMTP)
authAudit.checklist.smtp.ok: SMTP host and from-address are set.
authAudit.checklist.smtp.missing: Set SMTP under Dashboard Auth (needed for recovery / reset email).
authAudit.checklist.recovery.label: Recovery email
authAudit.checklist.recovery.ok: Verified recovery email on this super admin account.
authAudit.checklist.recovery.pending: Verification email sent — open the link to finish.
authAudit.checklist.recovery.unverified: Recovery email saved but not verified yet.
authAudit.checklist.recovery.missing: Add and verify a recovery email (My account).
authAudit.checklist.techPin.label: IT administrator PIN
authAudit.checklist.techPin.missing: Set IT administrator PIN on this page (must differ from login).
authAudit.checklist.audit.label: Audit trail
authAudit.checklist.audit.ok: Audit trail and CSV export are available — open to confirm.
healthPlain.okTitle: Dashboard, cameras, talk, and live video look ready.
healthPlain.notOkTitle: Something core is down. Check Settings or restart lab Start.
